area: Midtown Raleigh / North Hills District, NC
origin_note: Coordinates below are real lat/lng. local_xz is a simple flattened projection (meters, approx) using the district centroid as (0,0) -- good enough for placing meshes in a Three.js scene without needing a full map projection.
rotation_note: rotation_deg_est is an ESTIMATE, not surveyed data. It approximates how each building's long axis sits relative to the local street grid. 0 = long axis aligned with local north-south scene axis (+z), positive = clockwise rotation in degrees. Treat as a starting point for variety, not ground truth.

buildings.name: One North Hills Tower
buildings.notes: Tallest building in the cluster. Good anchor piece -- place it slightly off-center so it doesn't read as a single dominant spike.

buildings.name: Landmark at North Hills
buildings.notes: Clean boxy massing, simplest building to model -- reuse its mesh (scaled) for filler buildings elsewhere.

buildings.name: Bank of America Tower (4242 Six Forks Rd)
buildings.notes: Houses Regus/CapTrust office space and ground-floor restaurants (STIR, Yard House) in real life -- good candidate for a mixed-use base (retail at ground floor, office tower above).

buildings.name: North Hills Tower II
buildings.notes: Sits right beside Bank of America Tower -- pair them as a 'twin-ish' office cluster distinct from the Capital Towers residential twins.

buildings.name: Capital Towers
buildings.notes: Two near-identical towers side by side. Use InstancedMesh for the repeated window bands.

buildings.name: The Eastern
buildings.notes: One of the tallest residential points in the area, sits directly above the retail/park core. Good candidate for a second, slightly shorter hero tower overlooking the park.

buildings.name: Midtown Green Apartments
buildings.notes: Has balcony protrusions -- nice small geometric detail to add variety against flatter office towers.

buildings.name: Park & Market Apartments
buildings.notes: Ground-floor retail frontage facing the park/market street -- model with a slightly taller ground floor band.

buildings.name: Park Central North Hills Apartments
buildings.notes: Ground-floor retail space below residential floors, similar treatment to Park & Market.

buildings.name: The Dartmouth North Hills
buildings.notes: Standard mid-rise residential block near the office cluster.

buildings.name: RH Raleigh (showroom + rooftop restaurant)
buildings.notes: Distinctive 3-story showroom with a rooftop restaurant/terrace -- good candidate for a unique low-rise silhouette with a visible rooftop structure, breaking up box-on-box monotony.

buildings.name: North Hills Shopping/Mixed-Use District (general retail core)
buildings.rotation_note_local: This is a cluster, not one building -- vary individual filler-box rotations slightly (-10 to 10 deg) for an organic feel.
buildings.notes: Use several small varied-height boxes (1-3 stories) here. See shops.json for the specific named storefronts within this cluster.
